PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DISMISSED THEIR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND CIVIL BATTERY CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harold Gerecke, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and civil battery) and denied as to issue 5 (punitive damages). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation and civil battery causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL

Parties submitted to the Court's tentative, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s motion for judgment of dismissal is denied. C.C.P. 581(f) is discretionary, and the court refuses to exercise that discretion in this instance. Plaintiffs provided a sufficient excuse for their delay in amending the complaint after defendant's demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. (See Contreras v. Blue Cross of Calif.
